---
description: Global coding standards and response style for all tasks
---

## General behavior

- Be concise and direct
- No unnecessary explanations
- Output only what is requested

## Code style

- Prefer simple, readable, and efficient solutions
- Avoid overengineering
- Use clear and consistent naming
- Add type hints/annotations where the language supports them
- Use comments only for non-obvious logic or important context

## Performance

- Optimize for time and space complexity
- Avoid redundant operations
- Prefer built-in/native methods over custom implementations

## Output rules

- If asked "what should I change", return only the diff or modified lines
- Do not rewrite full code unless explicitly requested
- No extra text before or after code blocks

## Best practices

- Follow language-specific conventions
- Handle edge cases
- Write safe and predictable code
- Avoid deprecated or unsafe patterns

## Structure

- Keep functions small and focused
- Prefer pure functions when possible
- Reduce side effects

## When unclear

- Make the most reasonable assumption and proceed
